description_long: This object is a classic hand plane, typically used in woodworking for smoothing or shaping wood surfaces. It features a long, flat metal base with a sharp blade set at an angle in the center, which is adjusted by a knurled knob. The rear of the tool has an ergonomically shaped wooden handle, while the front sports a rounded wooden knob for guiding the tool during use. The body is primarily metallic, with visible screws and adjustment mechanisms, and the handles are made of polished wood. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, with a combination of dark metal and warm wood tones. This tool is commonly found in workshops, garages, or urban repair settings.
description: A robust metal hand plane with polished wooden handles and adjustment knobs, used for woodworking and surface smoothing.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the metal base, the angled blade, and both the rear handle and front knob.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the rounded wooden knob and the width of the metal base.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the first, with clear visibility of the handle and adjustment knob.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the ergonomic wooden handle and the alignment of the blade and adjustment mechanism.
